response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior full-stack engineer with broad web, mobile, and some systems experience, but the role's hard requirements are largely unmet: only brief professional C/C++ experience (six months at National Instruments), no demonstrated Linux kernel investigation or modification work, no embedded networking or LAN-switch background, no security-technology experience (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption), and critically no indication of Japanese language proficiency, which is required at native level. The profile is oriented toward web platforms and developer tooling rather than embedded firmware security.
strengths: 18+ years of software development experience across diverse stacks and domains
Some low-level systems exposure: C/C++ at National Instruments, low-latency DLang services, Linux proficiency
Strong engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling) and knowledge-sharing track record
concerns: No native-level Japanese proficiency indicated — a hard requirement for the role
Minimal professional C experience and no Linux kernel source-modification background
No embedded networking, LAN-switch, or security-protocol experience (MACsec, 802.1X, SNMP/NETCONF)

key_factors.factor: Lack of embedded/kernel and network-security experience
Broad senior engineering experience with some systems exposure
